mechanism_narrative: A high and non-declining federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 5.25% in the January 2007 vintage available by 2007-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is greater than -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment, reduced construction employment, and tighter household balance sheets, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1642 thousand units in the December 2006 vintage available by 2007-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST weakness is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases, or if the triggered mean return exceeds the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: Restrictive policy combined with falling housing starts transmits through mortgage credit and construction demand, lowering expected earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and HOUST declines more than 10% year over year
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 5.25% and HOUST was 1642 thousand units in the latest vintages available by 2007-02-15
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with housing-led demand slowdown
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-HOUST trigger does not produce lower 60-day returns than either single-series trigger alone, or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in a walk-forward logistic model.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment combined with expanding payrolls supports wage income and household spending, sustaining near-term corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% and PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.6% and PAYEMS was 137258 thousand in the January 2007 vintages available by 2007-02-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE and rising-PAYEMS condition is followed by below-baseline 20-day returns in out-of-sample tests, or if hit-rate for positive returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices while the policy rate is already restrictive signals reduced room for rate cuts and pressure on equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 4.0% while FEDFUNDS >= 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 202.8 in the December 2006 vintage and FEDFUNDS was 5.25% in the January 2007 vintage
failure_condition: Reject if high CPI momentum with FEDFUNDS >= 5.0% is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production improves the near-term earnings outlook for cyclical sectors and signals firmer real activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 111.9282 in the January 2007 vintage available by 2007-02-15
regime_hint: manufacturing expansion or soft-landing growth regime
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum has a walk-forward 5-day Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or if top-quintile INDPRO momentum underperforms bottom-quintile momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and falling revenue momentum for consumer-facing firms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 370418 million dollars in the January 2007 vintage available by 2007-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS contractions are not followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-contraction periods, or if triggered positive-return frequency exceeds the unconditional positive-return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: A material slowdown in real GDP growth signals weaker aggregate demand and lower expected corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth crosses below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 11541.6 billions of chained 2017 dollars in the October 2006 vintage available by 2007-02-15
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth below 1.0% annualized is followed by above-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, or if the downside signal has a nonnegative Rank IC.
